root_text.an4.178:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu aññataraṁ santaṁ cetovimuttiṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an4.178:1.5: Tassa sakkāyanirodhaṁ manasi karoto sakkāyanirodhe cittaṁ na pakkhandati nappasīdati na santiṭṭhati nādhimuccati.
root_text.an4.178:1.6: Tassa kho evaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno na sakkāyanirodho pāṭikaṅkho.
root_text.an4.178:1.7: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, puriso lepagatena hatthena sākhaṁ gaṇheyya, tassa so hattho sajjeyyapi gaṇheyyapi bajjheyyapi;
root_text.an4.178:1.8: evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu aññataraṁ santaṁ cetovimuttiṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an4.178:1.10: Tassa sakkāyanirodhaṁ manasi karoto sakkāyanirodhe cittaṁ na pakkhandati nappasīdati na santiṭṭhati nādhimuccati.
root_text.an4.178:1.11: Tassa kho evaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno na sakkāyanirodho pāṭikaṅkho.
root_text.an4.178:2.1: Idha pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhu aññataraṁ santaṁ cetovimuttiṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an4.178:2.3: Tassa sakkāyanirodhaṁ manasi karoto sakkāyanirodhe cittaṁ pakkhandati pasīdati santiṭṭhati adhimuccati.
root_text.an4.178:2.4: Tassa kho evaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno sakkāyanirodho pāṭikaṅkho.
root_text.an4.178:2.5: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, puriso suddhena hatthena sākhaṁ gaṇheyya, tassa so hattho neva sajjeyya na gaṇheyya na bajjheyya;
root_text.an4.178:2.6: evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu aññataraṁ santaṁ cetovimuttiṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an4.178:2.8: Tassa sakkāyanirodhaṁ manasi karoto sakkāyanirodhe cittaṁ pakkhandati pasīdati santiṭṭhati adhimuccati.
root_text.an4.178:2.9: Tassa kho evaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno sakkāyanirodho pāṭikaṅkho.
root_text.an4.178:3.1: Idha pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhu aññataraṁ santaṁ cetovimuttiṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an4.178:3.3: Tassa avijjāppabhedaṁ manasi karoto avijjāppabhede cittaṁ na pakkhandati nappasīdati na santiṭṭhati nādhimuccati.
root_text.an4.178:3.4: Tassa kho evaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno na avijjāppabhedo pāṭikaṅkho.
root_text.an4.178:3.6: Tassā puriso yāni ceva āyamukhāni tāni pidaheyya, yāni ca apāyamukhāni tāni vivareyya, devo ca na sammā dhāraṁ anuppaveccheyya.
root_text.an4.178:3.7: Evañhi tassā, bhikkhave, jambāliyā na āḷippabhedo pāṭikaṅkho.
root_text.an4.178:3.8: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu aññataraṁ santaṁ cetovimuttiṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an4.178:3.10: Tassa avijjāppabhedaṁ manasi karoto avijjāppabhede cittaṁ na pakkhandati nappasīdati na santiṭṭhati nādhimuccati.
root_text.an4.178:3.11: Tassa kho evaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno na avijjāppabhedo pāṭikaṅkho.
root_text.an4.178:4.1: Idha pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhu aññataraṁ santaṁ cetovimuttiṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an4.178:4.3: Tassa avijjāppabhedaṁ manasi karoto avijjāppabhede cittaṁ pakkhandati pasīdati santiṭṭhati adhimuccati.
root_text.an4.178:4.4: Tassa kho evaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno avijjāppabhedo pāṭikaṅkho.
root_text.an4.178:4.6: Tassā puriso yāni ceva āyamukhāni tāni vivareyya, yāni ca apāyamukhāni tāni pidaheyya, devo ca sammā dhāraṁ anuppaveccheyya.
root_text.an4.178:4.7: Evañhi tassā, bhikkhave, jambāliyā āḷippabhedo pāṭikaṅkho.
root_text.an4.178:4.8: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu aññataraṁ santaṁ cetovimuttiṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an4.178:4.10: Tassa avijjāppabhedaṁ manasi karoto avijjāppabhede cittaṁ pakkhandati pasīdati santiṭṭhati adhimuccati.
root_text.an4.178:4.11: Tassa kho evaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno avijjāppabhedo pāṭikaṅkho.
root_text.an4.178:4.12: Ime kho, bhikkhave, cattāro puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmin”ti.

translation_text.an4.178:1.1: “Mendicants, these four individuals are found in the world.
translation_text.an4.178:1.3: Take a mendicant who enters and remains in a peaceful release of the heart.
translation_text.an4.178:1.4: They focus on the cessation of substantial reality,
translation_text.an4.178:1.5: but on that their mind does not leap forth, gain confidence, settle down, and become decided.
translation_text.an4.178:1.6: You wouldn’t expect that mendicant to realize the cessation of substantial reality.
translation_text.an4.178:1.7: Suppose a person were to grab a branch with a glue-smeared hand. Their hand would stick, hold, and bind to it.
translation_text.an4.178:1.8: In the same way, take a mendicant who enters and remains in a peaceful release of the heart.
translation_text.an4.178:1.9: They focus on the cessation of substantial reality,
translation_text.an4.178:1.10: but on that their mind does not leap forth, gain confidence, settle down, and become decided.
translation_text.an4.178:1.11: You wouldn’t expect that mendicant to realize the cessation of substantial reality.
translation_text.an4.178:2.1: Next, take a mendicant who enters and remains in a peaceful release of the heart.
translation_text.an4.178:2.2: They focus on the cessation of substantial reality,
translation_text.an4.178:2.3: and on that their mind leaps forth, gains confidence, settles down, and becomes decided.
translation_text.an4.178:2.4: You would expect that mendicant to realize the cessation of substantial reality.
translation_text.an4.178:2.5: Suppose a person were to grab a branch with a clean hand. Their hand wouldn’t stick, hold, or bind to it.
translation_text.an4.178:2.6: In the same way, take a mendicant who enters and remains in a peaceful release of the heart.
translation_text.an4.178:2.7: They focus on the cessation of substantial reality,
translation_text.an4.178:2.8: and on that their mind leaps forth, gains confidence, settles down, and becomes decided.
translation_text.an4.178:2.9: You would expect that mendicant to realize the cessation of substantial reality.
translation_text.an4.178:3.1: Next, take a mendicant who enters and remains in a peaceful release of the heart.
translation_text.an4.178:3.3: but on that their mind does not leap forth, gain confidence, settle down, and become decided.
translation_text.an4.178:3.4: You wouldn’t expect that mendicant to smash ignorance.
translation_text.an4.178:3.5: Suppose there was a billabong that had been stagnant for many years.
translation_text.an4.178:3.6: And someone was to close off the inlets and open up the drains, and the heavens didn’t provide enough rain.
translation_text.an4.178:3.7: You wouldn’t expect that billabong to break its banks.
translation_text.an4.178:3.8: In the same way, take a mendicant who enters and remains in a certain peaceful release of the heart.
translation_text.an4.178:3.10: but on that their mind does not leap forth, gain confidence, settle down, and become decided.
translation_text.an4.178:3.11: You wouldn’t expect that mendicant to smash ignorance.
translation_text.an4.178:4.1: Next, take a mendicant who enters and remains in a peaceful release of the heart.
translation_text.an4.178:4.3: and on that their mind leaps forth, gains confidence, settles down, and becomes decided.
translation_text.an4.178:4.4: You would expect that mendicant to smash ignorance.
translation_text.an4.178:4.5: Suppose there was a billabong that had been stagnant for many years.
translation_text.an4.178:4.6: And someone was to open up the inlets and close off the drains, and the heavens provided plenty of rain.
translation_text.an4.178:4.7: You would expect that billabong to break its banks.
translation_text.an4.178:4.8: In the same way, take a mendicant who enters and remains in a certain peaceful release of the heart.
translation_text.an4.178:4.10: and on that their mind leaps forth, gains confidence, settles down, and becomes decided.
translation_text.an4.178:4.11: You would expect that mendicant to smash ignorance.
translation_text.an4.178:4.12: These are the four individuals found in the world.”

variant_text.an4.178:1.6: Tassa kho evaṁ → tassa kho etaṁ (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed); evaṁ kho tassa (?)
variant_text.an4.178:1.7: lepagatena → lasagatena (bj, pts1ed); lapagatena (sya-all) | gaṇheyyapi → gaṇheyyāpi (pts1ed); gayheyyapi (?) | bajjheyyapi → khajjeyyapi (bj); bajjheyyāpi (pts1ed)
variant_text.an4.178:1.11: Tassa kho evaṁ → tassa kho etaṁ (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed); evaṁ kho tassa (?)
